explanationZhTw: `cubicle` 指隔間、隔板小空間；這裡是辦公/工作區中的小隔間。
generatedExample: Winston returned to his cubicle after a short break.

explanationZhTw: `lavatory` 是較正式或較舊式的「廁所、洗手間」說法。
generatedExample: He asked where the lavatory was before the meeting started.

explanationZhTw: solitary 這裡是「孤單的、獨自的」意思，用來形容只有一個人的狀態。
generatedExample: He lived a solitary life in a small cabin.

explanationZhTw: run into her 這裡是「偶然遇見她、碰上她」，不是字面上的「跑進去」。
generatedExample: I ran into an old classmate at the market.

explanationZhTw: in a sling 在這裡是指「手臂吊著固定帶」，表示受傷後被吊帶支撐住。
generatedExample: Her arm was in a sling after the accident.

explanationZhTw: roughed in 這裡是專門語境用法，意思是「粗略勾畫出、先畫出雛形」。
generatedExample: The architect roughed in the floor plan on a napkin.

explanationZhTw: 「flat on her face」是慣用說法，表示整個人臉朝下重重摔倒。
generatedExample: He slipped on the wet floor and fell flat on his face.

explanationZhTw: 「wrung out of her」在這裡不是字面上的「擰乾」，而是指某種反應被痛得不由自主地逼出來。
generatedExample: The unexpected news wrung a sigh out of her.

explanationZhTw: 「stop short」表示突然停住，常用來形容因驚訝、警覺或意外而立刻停下。
generatedExample: She stopped short when she saw the broken window.

explanationZhTw: 「milky yellow」是帶有不自然、蒼白感的顏色描述，這裡用來強調臉色病態。
generatedExample: After the long illness, his skin had a milky yellow colour.

explanationZhTw: 在這裡不是指「攪動」東西，而是指情緒在心中「浮現、被喚起」。
generatedExample: The sight of the old photograph stirred memories he had long forgotten.

explanationZhTw: 指「敵人、仇敵」；這裡特別強調對方是會殺他的人。
generatedExample: They treated the soldier as an enemy, not as a guest.

explanationZhTw: 這是較不尋常的說法，指「一個人／一個人類」，帶有把對方看成生物個體的冷峻感。
generatedExample: The doctor saw not a case file, but a human creature who was terrified.

explanationZhTw: 表示「在痛苦中、感到疼痛」；這裡是描述受傷後的身體狀態。
generatedExample: After the fall, she was in pain but still able to walk.

explanationZhTw: 表示「出於本能地、下意識地」；不是經過思考才行動。
generatedExample: She instinctively reached for his hand when she heard the loud noise.

explanationZhTw: 表示「彷彿、好像」，用來把一種感受比作另一種情況。
generatedExample: He stared at the empty room as though he had lost something precious.

explanationZhTw: `hurt` 在這裡是形容詞，意思是「受傷的、身體有傷痛的」。這是日常常見用法，屬於 A2 級字彙。
generatedExample: She was hurt after falling off her bike.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `nothing` 不是字面上的「沒有東西」，而是表示「沒什麼大不了的、不要緊」；用來安撫對方。
generatedExample: Don't worry about the cut. It's nothing.

explanationZhTw: `all right` 這裡表示「沒事、會好的」，不是「全部都對」；常用來安慰或描述狀況恢復正常。
generatedExample: Take a deep breath. You'll be all right soon.

explanationZhTw: 片語 `in a second` 表示「馬上、很快地」，通常指不久之後。
generatedExample: I just need to finish this email, and I'll call you in a second.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是指真的「飛舞」，而是形容心跳很快、因緊張或激動而怦怦跳。
generatedExample: Her heart was fluttering before she gave the speech.

explanationZhTw: 這是固定的描述說法，意思是「臉色變得很蒼白」，通常表示吃驚、害怕或身體不適。
generatedExample: When he heard the news, he turned very pale.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `broken` 是 `break` 的過去分詞，意思是「弄壞、損壞」。整句是在確認對方有沒有造成任何東西受損。
generatedExample: I hope I haven't broken your phone.

explanationZhTw: 「all right」在這裡表示「沒事、還好、不要緊」，不是在表達「正確」或「可以」。
generatedExample: I'm all right now, so you don't need to worry.

explanationZhTw: 「hurt」在這裡是動詞，意思是「疼、受傷、感到痛」。
generatedExample: My knee hurt after I ran up the stairs.

explanationZhTw: 「for a moment」表示「片刻、一下子、短時間內」。
generatedExample: She was silent for a moment before answering.

explanationZhTw: 「that's all」表示「就這樣而已、僅此而已」，用來強調沒有更多情況。
generatedExample: I only need a pen, that's all.

explanationZhTw: `held out` 在這裡是「伸出、遞出」的意思，指她把手伸向他。
generatedExample: She held out her hand to greet me.

explanationZhTw: `helped her up` 是「扶她起來」的意思，表示幫助某人從坐著或跌倒的狀態站起來。
generatedExample: He helped her up after she slipped.

explanationZhTw: `regained some of her colour` 不是字面上的「恢復顏色」而已，而是指她看起來比較有血色、精神變好了。
generatedExample: After resting, he regained some of his colour and looked stronger.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `shortly` 不是「不久之後」，而是指說話簡短、帶點不耐煩或冷淡地回答。
generatedExample: He answered shortly and walked away.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `give ... a bang` 表示把手腕撞到或敲到一下；`a bit of` 表示「有點、稍微」，整句是在說手腕只是輕微受撞。
generatedExample: I gave my elbow a bit of a bang on the doorframe.

explanationZhTw: `comrade` 指「同志、同伴、夥伴」；在這裡是帶有親近感，也有一點政治語感的稱呼。
generatedExample: Thanks, comrade, for helping me move the boxes.

explanationZhTw: 「walked on」在這裡是「繼續往前走、走開」的意思。
generatedExample: She waved once and walked on without looking back.

explanationZhTw: 「incident」指「事件、插曲」，這裡是指剛剛發生的那件事。
generatedExample: The incident at school was over quickly.

explanationZhTw: 這個片語表示「已經變得像本能一樣自然」。不是字面上真的變成本能，而是強調長期養成的自我控制。
generatedExample: For her, checking the door twice had acquired the status of an instinct.

explanationZhTw: 「in any case」是口語常用語，意思是「無論如何、反正、總之」。
generatedExample: I was tired, but in any case I finished the report.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「betray」不是「背叛」，而是「不自覺流露、顯露出」；整句是指「無法完全掩飾一瞬間的驚訝」。
generatedExample: His voice betrayed a momentary surprise when he heard the news.

explanationZhTw: 「slip something into someone's hand」表示「偷偷、迅速把某物塞進某人手裡」。
generatedExample: He slipped a note into my hand before the meeting started.

explanationZhTw: 「intentionally」意思是「故意地、有意地」。
generatedExample: She dropped the glass intentionally to get everyone’s attention.

explanationZhTw: 「passed through」在這裡是「穿過、經過」的意思。
generatedExample: We passed through the lobby on our way out.

explanationZhTw: 「lavatory」是較正式或英式的說法，意思是「廁所、洗手間」。
generatedExample: I asked where the lavatory was before boarding the train.

explanationZhTw: 「scrap of paper」指「一小片紙、紙屑、碎紙片」。
generatedExample: She found a scrap of paper with an old phone number on it.

explanationZhTw: 小便斗；男性在公共廁所使用的便器。
generatedExample: He waited at the urinal until the stall was free.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是「管理」，而是「設法做到；成功完成」。
generatedExample: She managed to finish the report before noon.

explanationZhTw: 這裡指「用手指撥弄、操作」，不是其他抽象意思。
generatedExample: He opened the tiny clasp by fingering it carefully.

explanationZhTw: be tempted to + 動詞：很想做某事，但知道不一定合適。
generatedExample: I was tempted to call in sick and stay home.

explanationZhTw: 舊式說法，指廁所或洗手間。
generatedExample: The old hotel still had signs that said 'Water Closet'.

explanationZhTw: 這裡表示「立刻、馬上」。
generatedExample: Please come here at once.

explanationZhTw: folly 指「愚蠢的行為」；整個片語表示「極其愚蠢的舉動」。
generatedExample: Ignoring the warning would be shocking folly.

explanationZhTw: 這裡指「持續被監視、一直被盯著看」；帶有被動監控的意思。
generatedExample: The cameras watched the entrance continuously.

explanationZhTw: 辦公隔間；這裡指狹小的工作格間。
generatedExample: She worked quietly in a small cubicle at the back of the office.

explanationZhTw: 眼鏡；較正式、偏文學的說法。
generatedExample: He took off his spectacles and rubbed his eyes.

explanationZhTw: 把打字機往自己這邊拉近；這裡的 hitched 是「猛地拉動／移近」，不是常見的其他意思。
generatedExample: She hitched the chair closer to the table before starting work.

explanationZhTw: 至少、起碼；用來強調最低限度。
generatedExample: We need at the very least one more hour to finish the task.

explanationZhTw: 此處表示「正在從事、處理」某項工作。
generatedExample: She was engaged on a difficult report all morning.

explanationZhTw: 只是例行公事、普通流程；強調這工作很常規、不費心。
generatedExample: For him, the weekly update was mere routine.

explanationZhTw: 更正、修正；屬較正式用語。
generatedExample: The rectification of the error took only a few minutes.

explanationZhTw: 「Thought Police 的特務／線人」；這裡的 agent 不是一般的「代理人」，而是秘密警察的情報員、臥底。
generatedExample: The novel makes the agent of the regime sound ordinary until the danger becomes clear.

explanationZhTw: 以這種方式送達；`in such a fashion` 是較正式的說法，意思接近「用這種方式」。
generatedExample: They filed the report in such a fashion that no one noticed the missing pages.

explanationZhTw: 傳喚令、召喚令；在這裡是法律或權力機關要求對方出面的正式通知。
generatedExample: The letter was a summons to appear in court next Monday.

explanationZhTw: 不斷冒出來、反覆浮現；`raise its head` 在此是比喻，表示念頭一再出現。
generatedExample: A bad habit kept raising its head whenever she was stressed.

explanationZhTw: 地下組織；指秘密運作、反抗當權者的團體，不是字面上的「在地下」。
generatedExample: The underground organization printed banned books in secret.

explanationZhTw: 「兄弟會」；在這部小說裡是反抗體制的秘密組織名稱，屬於專有名詞。
generatedExample: He kept asking whether the Brotherhood was real or just propaganda.

explanationZhTw: 突然想到；`spring into one's mind` 表示某個念頭瞬間出現。
generatedExample: The answer sprang into my mind while I was walking home.

explanationZhTw: 「口述寫字機」；這是小說中的特殊裝置，通常是把口說內容直接記錄下來的機器。
generatedExample: He dictated the memo into the speakwrite instead of typing it.

explanationZhTw: 「rolled up」在這裡是把一疊東西捲起、收攏成一束。
generatedExample: She rolled up the poster and put it in a tube.

explanationZhTw: 「slid it into」在這裡是把它滑進、塞進某個地方，動作很順。
generatedExample: He slid the letter into the envelope.

explanationZhTw: 「pneumatic tube」是氣動輸送管，利用氣流把文件或小物件送到別處的管道。
generatedExample: The office sent forms through a pneumatic tube.

explanationZhTw: 「batch of work」在這裡不是一般「一批貨」的意思，而是指一疊、一起處理的一批工作文件。
generatedExample: I finished the first batch of work before lunch.

explanationZhTw: 「scrap of paper」指一小張、一小片紙。
generatedExample: He wrote the number on a scrap of paper.

explanationZhTw: 「flattened it out」是把它壓平、攤平。
generatedExample: She flattened out the map on the table.

explanationZhTw: 「unformed handwriting」在這裡指字跡還沒有固定風格，通常帶有潦草、歪斜或不成熟的感覺。
generatedExample: The note was written in a large unformed handwriting.

explanationZhTw: `love` 是「愛、喜愛」的意思；這裡是直接表達情感。
generatedExample: I love my family very much.

explanationZhTw: 「stunned」表示「震驚得愣住了、腦袋一時反應不過來」。這裡不是指被撞暈，而是指短暫地因驚訝而無法立刻行動。
generatedExample: I was stunned by the news and forgot what I was saying.

explanationZhTw: 「incriminating」表示「足以證明有罪的、會讓人惹上麻煩的」。這裡用來形容那個東西可能成為不利證據。
generatedExample: She deleted the incriminating message before anyone saw it.

explanationZhTw: 「memory hole」在《一九八四》裡是專有的反烏托邦用語，指用來銷毀文件或證據的管道，不是一般字面上的「記憶洞」。
generatedExample: The report vanished into the memory hole before the public could read it.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是動詞，意思是「集中注意力、專注於」。
generatedExample: I need to focus on my homework before dinner.

explanationZhTw: 意思是「隱藏、掩飾」；這裡指刻意不讓別人看出他的焦躁。
generatedExample: She tried to conceal her excitement, but she was smiling too much.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是「運動」或「攪動」，而是指「焦躁、不安的情緒」。
generatedExample: He could not hide his agitation after hearing the news.

explanationZhTw: 這是《1984》中的特定裝置，指帶有監視功能的螢幕，不是一般單純的電視。
generatedExample: Everyone spoke carefully because the telescreen might be listening.

explanationZhTw: 這是慣用語，意思是「不巧地、偏偏」。
generatedExample: As bad luck would have it, the train left just as we arrived.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是貶抑用法，指「蠢人、笨蛋」。
generatedExample: He muttered that the driver was an imbecile after the near miss.

explanationZhTw: 這是片語動詞，意思是「重重地坐下、一下子癱坐下來」。
generatedExample: She flopped down on the sofa after a long day.

explanationZhTw: 這裡指「刺鼻的氣味」或「濃烈的味道」，不是一般的「少許滋味」。
generatedExample: There was a tang of vinegar in the sauce.

explanationZhTw: 這裡形容氣味像金屬一樣單薄、刺鼻；不是常見的「錫製的」。
generatedExample: The room had a tinny smell after the paint dried.

explanationZhTw: 這是片語動詞，意思是「持續進行、繼續不斷」。
generatedExample: They kept up the discussion until midnight.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是「球拍」，而是指「吵雜聲、喧鬧聲」。
generatedExample: We could not sleep בגלל the racket outside.

explanationZhTw: 意思是「愚蠢的、空洞的」，常帶有輕蔑語氣。
generatedExample: He made a fatuous comment that annoyed everyone in the room.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是名詞/動詞，意思是「一瞥、瞥見」。
generatedExample: I caught a glimpse of him before he disappeared into the crowd.

explanationZhTw: 「bearable」表示「可以忍受的、沒那麼難熬的」。
generatedExample: The pain was bad, but it was bearable.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「delicate」不是「精緻」，而是「棘手、需要小心處理的」。
generatedExample: It was a delicate matter, so she chose her words carefully.

explanationZhTw: 「necessitated」表示「使得有必要、迫使必須」。
generatedExample: The storm necessitated a change in our travel plans.

explanationZhTw: 「cast discredit on...」是固定說法，表示「使...蒙羞、讓...失去信譽」。
generatedExample: The scandal cast discredit on the whole organization.

explanationZhTw: 「under a cloud」表示「名譽受損、處於嫌疑或負面評價中」。
generatedExample: After the accusation, the manager was under a cloud.

explanationZhTw: 表示「完全把她從腦中排除、刻意不去想她」。
generatedExample: He tried shutting the memory of the argument out of his mind.

explanationZhTw: 「think ... out」表示「把事情想清楚、徹底思考」。
generatedExample: We need to think the plan out before we announce it.

explanationZhTw: 「wolfed」在這裡是「狼吞虎嚥地吃」的意思，帶有又快又急的語氣。
generatedExample: He wolfed down the pizza in five minutes.

explanationZhTw: 「take part in」表示「參與、加入」。
generatedExample: Everyone took part in the discussion.

explanationZhTw: 這裡指「一本正經卻很荒唐的鬧劇／蠢事」，帶有諷刺意味。
generatedExample: The interview felt like solemn foolery rather than a serious process.

explanationZhTw: 「shirk」表示「逃避、偷懶不做應做的事」。
generatedExample: He tried to shirk his responsibilities, but his boss noticed.

explanationZhTw: 「welled up」表示情緒或感覺「湧上來」。
generatedExample: Tears welled up in her eyes when she heard the news.

explanationZhTw: 這是《1984》裡的虛構裝置，類似可監看、可廣播的螢幕／監視電視。
generatedExample: In the novel, the telescreen is always watching the citizens.

explanationZhTw: 指「聯絡、取得聯繫」；這裡是要設法和那個女孩接上線。
generatedExample: I'll get in touch with you tomorrow morning.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 trap 不是字面上的「陷阱」，而是指她可能在設計圈套、騙他上當。
generatedExample: He worried that the offer was laying some kind of trap for him.

explanationZhTw: 慣用語，表示「嚇壞了、怕得要命」。
generatedExample: The sudden noise frightened me out of my wits.

explanationZhTw: 在這裡 advances 指「示好、追求、搭訕」的舉動，不是一般的「前進」。
generatedExample: She ignored his advances and walked away.

explanationZhTw: 表示「突然想到、掠過腦海」。
generatedExample: It never crossed his mind to apologize.

explanationZhTw: 在這裡表示「從他身邊溜走、失去她」；帶有失去掌控的意味。
generatedExample: He felt the opportunity slip away from him.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `whereabouts` 指「所在位置、下落」，比單純的 `where` 更書面，也帶有不確定地點的意思。
generatedExample: I have no idea about the whereabouts of my passport.

explanationZhTw: `pretext` 是「藉口、托詞」，表示表面理由和真正目的不同。
generatedExample: He left early on the pretext of having a dentist appointment.

explanationZhTw: `contrived` 在這裡不是「做作」的貶義，而是「費心安排、設法做到」。
generatedExample: They contrived to finish the report before the deadline.

explanationZhTw: `loitering about` 指在某地無所事事地徘徊、逗留。
generatedExample: He was loitering about outside the shop after closing time.

explanationZhTw: 慣用語，表示「完全不可能；根本不用考慮」。
generatedExample: Going there tonight is out of the question because of the storm.

explanationZhTw: `in transit` 指「在運送途中、轉運途中」，這裡是信件在郵寄過程中。
generatedExample: The monitor was damaged in transit.

explanationZhTw: `inapplicable` 指「不適用的、不合用的」，常用於規則、條款或選項。
generatedExample: Delete any options that are inapplicable to your situation.

explanationZhTw: 慣用語，表示「更不用說」；前面較難做到，後面更不用提。
generatedExample: I can't afford a new car, let alone a holiday abroad.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `buzz` 不是昆蟲嗡嗡叫，而是指一片低低的交談聲、背景人聲的喧鬧感。
generatedExample: There was a buzz of conversation before the meeting started.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `shift` 指的是工作班次；`later shift` 就是「較晚的班次／晚一點上班的時段」。
generatedExample: I was moved to a later shift, so I do not get off until midnight.

explanationZhTw: `without a glance` 是固定說法，表示「連看一眼都沒有」；強調兩個人刻意不互相理會。
generatedExample: She walked by without a glance, as if he were a stranger.

explanationZhTw: `at all` 在否定句裡用來加強語氣，意思是「完全、絲毫」。
generatedExample: He did not call me at all yesterday.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `transparency` 不是字面上的「透明」，而是指一種「毫無遮掩、完全暴露」的感覺。
generatedExample: After the lie was exposed, he felt a painful transparency in every conversation.

explanationZhTw: `an agony` 指極度痛苦、折磨；在這裡是情緒和感官上的強烈煎熬。
generatedExample: Waiting for the test results was an agony for her.

explanationZhTw: `at a stretch` 表示「連續不間斷地」或「一口氣地」。
generatedExample: I can focus for only twenty minutes at a stretch.

explanationZhTw: `no clue` 是口語說法，意思是「完全不知道、毫無頭緒」。
generatedExample: I had no clue where I left my keys.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `vaporized` 不是單純的「蒸發」，而是帶有極權世界語境中的「被抹除／被消滅」之意。
generatedExample: In the story, the dissident was vaporized without a trace.

explanationZhTw: `changed her mind` 是常見慣用語，意思是「改變主意」。
generatedExample: She changed her mind and stayed home instead of going out.

explanationZhTw: 表示「再次出現、重新露面」，在這裡是說她隔天又出現了。
generatedExample: She reappeared at the station the next morning.

explanationZhTw: 指「吊帶；手臂吊帶」，用來支撐受傷的手臂。
generatedExample: After the fall, he wore his arm in a sling for two weeks.

explanationZhTw: 英式說法，指「膠布、OK 繃」一類的貼布。
generatedExample: She put a piece of sticking-plaster over the cut.

explanationZhTw: 片語動詞，這裡是「被耽擱、被卡住」的意思。
generatedExample: Our train was held up by signal problems.

explanationZhTw: 這裡指「一顆糖精錠／代糖錠」，是可放入口中的甜味替代品。
generatedExample: He dropped a tablet of saccharine into his tea.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 secured 不是「保護」，而是「順利拿到、取得」托盤。
generatedExample: She secured her tray before looking for a seat.

explanationZhTw: 片語動詞，表示「朝某處走去、前往」。
generatedExample: We made for the exit when the alarm started.

explanationZhTw: vacant 在這裡是「空著的、未被占用的」。
generatedExample: There was a vacant seat next to the window.

explanationZhTw: 指「沒有人陪伴／看顧的」。
generatedExample: The child was left unattended for a few minutes.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 beamed 不是「發射光束」，而是「露出燦爛笑容地看著」；語氣帶點誇張。
generatedExample: The teacher beamed into the classroom when the results were announced.

explanationZhTw: 指「幻覺」，也就是明明沒發生卻像真的一樣出現在腦中的影像或感受。
generatedExample: He had a hallucination after being awake for two nights.

explanationZhTw: 片語動詞，這裡是「坐滿、變得滿了」的意思。
generatedExample: The room filled up quickly before the talk began.

explanationZhTw: 「take the hint」是慣用語，意思是「領會暗示、看懂對方的意思」。這裡指她可能會注意到他的意圖，自己主動配合。
generatedExample: If he keeps looking at the door, she will take the hint and leave.

explanationZhTw: 「queue」是名詞，指「隊伍、排隊的人龍」。在英式英語很常見。
generatedExample: We waited in a long queue for concert tickets.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「making straight for」不是字面上的「做直線」，而是指「直接朝某處走去、徑直前往」。
generatedExample: He made straight for the exit when the meeting ended.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「sank」是比喻用法，表示「希望再次落空、心情再次低落」。
generatedExample: My confidence sank again after the second mistake.

explanationZhTw: 「vacant」在這裡表示「空的、沒有人使用的」，所以「vacant place」就是「空位」。
generatedExample: There was a vacant place near the window.

explanationZhTw: 這是慣用性的比喻，表示「心裡發冷、非常緊張或害怕」。
generatedExample: She listened to the bad news with ice at her heart.

explanationZhTw: 「malignant」平常常見義是「惡性的、致命的」，但這裡用來形容眼神，表示「充滿惡意的、兇狠的」。
generatedExample: She gave him a malignant glance before walking away.

explanationZhTw: 「trip someone up」是片語動詞，意思是「絆倒某人」；也可引申為「使某人出錯」。這裡是字面上的絆倒。
generatedExample: I tripped him up as he ran past the table.

explanationZhTw: 「thundering heart」是誇飾性的說法，表示「心臟跳得很快、非常緊張或激動」。
generatedExample: With a thundering heart, he opened the envelope.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 promptly 表示「立刻、毫不拖延地」，語氣比 quickly 更正式。
generatedExample: She promptly replied to the email.

explanationZhTw: 這個片語表示「完全支配、讓某種情緒控制住」；不是字面上的「佔有」。
generatedExample: A sudden panic took possession of her.

explanationZhTw: go by 在這裡表示「時間過去」。
generatedExample: Three months went by before we heard from them.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 altogether 不是「總共」，而是「完全、乾脆」；表示他本來可能完全不開口。
generatedExample: I would have stopped altogether if you hadn't called.

explanationZhTw: watery stuff 這裡不是單純的「東西」，而是帶有貶義地指那種稀薄、沒什麼料的食物。
generatedExample: We were served watery stuff for dinner.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是報時用法，指「18:30」或「晚上 6:30」。在一般口語裡常用來直接說時間點，和字面上的「一八三十」不同。
generatedExample: We need to leave by eighteen-thirty if we want to catch the train.

explanationZhTw: 「meet」在這裡是「碰面、見面」的意思，屬於常見的 A2 以上用語。
generatedExample: Let's meet at the library after school.

explanationZhTw: 「telescreens」是《1984》中的特定名詞，指裝有螢幕與監控功能的電視監視器。這裡不是一般的「電視」，而是帶有監視意味的專有詞彙。
generatedExample: The room had a telescreen in the corner, so we spoke quietly.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `doesn't matter` 表示「不重要、無所謂」，不是指「物質」的 `matter`。
generatedExample: It doesn't matter whether we start now or in ten minutes.

explanationZhTw: `crowd` 指「人群、很多人聚在一起」，是日常常見字。
generatedExample: There was a crowd outside the station after the concert.

explanationZhTw: `signal` 在這裡是「訊號；消息」的意思，指是否有收到可辨識的通知或反應。
generatedExample: She waited for any signal that the train was approaching.

explanationZhTw: `come up to` 在這裡是「走近、靠近某人」的意思。整句是在說，先不要靠近我，除非你在人群中看到我。
generatedExample: Please don't come up to me while I'm talking to someone else.

explanationZhTw: `look at` 表示「看著、注視」。這裡是叫對方不要看自己。
generatedExample: Look at the picture on the wall.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `keep` 不是「保留」，而是「待在、保持在某個位置」。整句是在說「你就待在我附近」。
generatedExample: Keep near me when the crowd gets bigger.

explanationZhTw: 這是口語中詢問現在幾點的說法，意思是「現在幾點？」
generatedExample: What time is the meeting?

explanationZhTw: `hours` 指「小時」，這裡是最基本的時間單位；整句只是表示「十九小時」。
generatedExample: We waited for three hours before the train arrived.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是口語中的回應，表示「好、可以、行」，用來接受對方的話或表示同意，不是字面上的「全部都對」。
generatedExample: "All right," she said, and opened the door.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `see` 不是「用眼睛看見」的意思，而是「注意到、察覺到」。整個片語表示 Ampleforth 沒有注意到 Winston。
generatedExample: I failed to see my friend in the crowd and walked right past him.

explanationZhTw: `make off` 是片語動詞，意思是「匆忙離開、溜走」。這裡表示那個女孩很快就離開了。
generatedExample: After the argument, he made off before anyone could ask questions.

explanationZhTw: 指「約定／預定的時間」；這裡是說他比原定時間還早到。
generatedExample: She arrived at the appointed time and started the meeting on schedule.

explanationZhTw: 指「四處閒晃、漫無目的地繞著走」；`round` 在這裡是「四周、到處」的意思。
generatedExample: We wandered round the museum for an hour before choosing a gallery to visit.

explanationZhTw: 表示「徹底擊敗、戰勝」；比一般的 `defeat` 更正式、帶有強烈勝利感。
generatedExample: The army vanquished its enemies after a long and costly battle.

explanationZhTw: 指「突然攫住、緊緊抓住」；這裡是說恐懼一下子襲上心頭。
generatedExample: A strange panic seized upon the crowd when the lights went out.

explanationZhTw: 在這裡是「聚集起來、累積到一起」；指周圍的人數變多了。
generatedExample: More customers had accumulated outside the shop before it opened.

explanationZhTw: 建築學用語，指「山牆、三角楣飾」；這裡是紀念碑或建築上方的裝飾部位。
generatedExample: The statue stood beneath the pediment of the old courthouse.

explanationZhTw: 指「車隊、護送隊」；這裡是一起移動、通常受保護的一群車輛或人。
generatedExample: A convoy of trucks moved slowly through the mountain pass.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是字面上的「受重力吸引」，而是指「自然地傾向待在某個地方」；整句是在說 Winston 平常會先站在圈子外圍。
generatedExample: She gravitates to the outer edge of a group when she first meets new people.

explanationZhTw: 這裡指一群人推擠、混亂的場面，不是嚴格的運動語境。
generatedExample: A small scrimmage broke out near the ticket gate.

explanationZhTw: 表示「近到伸手就碰得到」，是一種固定說法。
generatedExample: The car passed within arm's length of the wall.

explanationZhTw: 在《1984》裡是特定的階級稱呼，指無產階級或底層群眾，不是一般日常用語。
generatedExample: The novel uses the word prole to describe ordinary workers.

explanationZhTw: 這是誇張比喻，意思是兩個人挨得太緊、擋住去路，幾乎穿不過去。
generatedExample: The crowd formed an impenetrable wall of flesh around the stage.

explanationZhTw: wriggle 在這裡表示「扭動身體擠過去」，帶有費力閃躲的畫面。
generatedExample: He wriggled himself sideways through the narrow gap.

explanationZhTw: 指突然向前猛撲或猛衝一步。
generatedExample: She made a quick lunge for the ball.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是極度誇張的說法，表示被擠壓得非常痛苦，像果肉一樣爛成糊狀。
generatedExample: My legs felt ground to pulp after the long hike.

explanationZhTw: phrasal verb，指「突破阻擋、穿過去」。
generatedExample: They finally broke through the crowd.

explanationZhTw: 固定片語，表示人與人靠得很近、肩並肩站著。
generatedExample: The players stood shoulder to shoulder for the anthem.

explanationZhTw: 「sub-machine guns」是「衝鋒槍、機關槍類的連發槍械」。這裡用來強調守衛火力武裝很重。
generatedExample: The police arrived with sub-machine guns after the hostage call.

explanationZhTw: 「shabby」在這裡表示「破舊的、寒酸的」，不是指人的態度，而是指制服看起來很舊、很差。
generatedExample: He wore a shabby coat that had seen many winters.

explanationZhTw: 「leg-irons」是「腳鐐」，指套在腳踝上的金屬拘束器具。
generatedExample: The prisoners were chained in leg-irons during transport.

explanationZhTw: 「intermittently」表示「間歇地、斷斷續續地」，不是一直持續發生。
generatedExample: The signal worked intermittently during the storm.

explanationZhTw: 「take charge of」是片語動詞，意思是「接管、掌控、負責處理」。
generatedExample: When the manager left, Maya took charge of the meeting.

explanationZhTw: 「expressionless」表示「沒有表情的、面無表情的」。這裡是形容聲音冷淡、沒有情緒。
generatedExample: He answered in an expressionless voice and said nothing else.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「drowned by」不是字面上的「淹死」，而是指聲音被更大的噪音蓋過，聽不見。
generatedExample: Her apology was drowned by the noise of the train.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `get ... off` 指「請到假／把某段時間空出來不用上班或上學」。整句是在問對方能不能在星期天下午請假或不排班。
generatedExample: I asked my manager if I could get Monday morning off for a doctor’s appointment.

explanationZhTw: 「仔細聽」表示要專心聽對方說話；這裡是直接的命令句，用來強調接下來的資訊很重要。
generatedExample: Listen carefully to the instructions before you start.

explanationZhTw: 「have to」表示「必須、得要」，帶有義務或必要性，比單純的意願更強。
generatedExample: You have to show your ID at the entrance.

explanationZhTw: 「remember」是「記得、記住」；這裡指把剛剛聽到的內容牢記在心。
generatedExample: Please remember the password for tomorrow.

explanationZhTw: 「go to」在這裡是「前往、去到」的意思，屬於指示某個目的地的基本用法。
generatedExample: Go to the station and wait by the ticket gate.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是字面上的「軍事」，而是形容她做事非常有條理、非常精準，幾乎沒有一點誤差。
generatedExample: She organized the files with military precision.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 outlined 不是「畫出輪廓」，而是「清楚說明、列出」。
generatedExample: The teacher outlined the main points of the lesson.

explanationZhTw: 指長滿草的巷道或小路，暗示這條路很少有人走、已經荒廢。
generatedExample: We followed a grass-grown lane behind the old barn.

explanationZhTw: 這是比喻，意思是她對路線記得非常清楚，腦中像有地圖一樣。
generatedExample: After one visit, he seemed to have a map inside his head.

explanationZhTw: 指低聲說、喃喃地說，聲音很小而且不太清楚。
generatedExample: She murmured a quick apology before leaving.

explanationZhTw: 在這裡，`turn left` 是指示方向的常用說法，意思是「向左轉」。
generatedExample: At the corner, turn left and walk two blocks.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `got` 不是「得到」，而是口語中的 `has`，意思是「有」；`gate's got no top bar` 就是「那扇門沒有上橫桿」。
generatedExample: My bike's got no light, so I can't ride after dark.

explanationZhTw: `have to` 表示「不得不、必須」，這裡是在說可能需要等一下，帶有義務或必要性的語氣。
generatedExample: If the train is late, we may have to wait for an hour.

explanationZhTw: `get there` 在這裡不是字面上的「到那裡」，而是指「到達某個地方」；屬於常見的口語用法。
generatedExample: We can get there before noon if we leave now.

explanationZhTw: 最常見的肯定回答，表示「是的」或「對」。這裡是單獨作為回應使用，屬於很基礎的 A2 詞彙。
generatedExample: "Yes," she said, and opened the door.

explanationZhTw: phrasal verb，意思是「離開、走開、離遠一點」。這裡帶有命令口氣，表示要對方立刻遠離說話者。
generatedExample: Please get away from the edge of the platform.

explanationZhTw: 把自己從人群中脫身、掙脫出來。
generatedExample: We tried to extricate ourselves from the traffic jam before sunset.

explanationZhTw: 排成一列慢慢經過，像隊伍一樣往前移動。
generatedExample: Students were filing past the stage to receive their certificates.

explanationZhTw: 貪看不厭地；怎麼看都不夠地。
generatedExample: The audience watched insatiably as the magician revealed each trick.

explanationZhTw: 當時最主要、占上風的情緒。
generatedExample: The prevailing emotion in the room was relief after the announcement.

explanationZhTw: 以……的外表／身分出現；表面上看起來像。
generatedExample: He entered the meeting in the guise of a consultant.

explanationZhTw: 短暫的一瞥。
generatedExample: I caught a momentary glimpse of her in the crowd.

explanationZhTw: 消失了；這裡帶有「再也找不到、下落不明」的意思。
generatedExample: The lights vanished as the storm moved away.

explanationZhTw: 接近結束、快要完了。
generatedExample: Our conversation was drawing to an end when the phone rang.

explanationZhTw: 把他們包圍住、卡住，讓他們難以離開。
generatedExample: Tall buildings hemmed us in on both sides of the narrow street.

explanationZhTw: 「緊緊握在一起」；這裡形容雙手互相緊握不放。
generatedExample: They sat quietly with their fingers clasped together.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是「探險」，而是「細細摸索、逐一感受」的意思，特別指用觸覺仔細辨認。
generatedExample: She explored the texture of the fabric with her fingertips.

explanationZhTw: 「因長期勞作而變得粗硬的」；常用來形容手掌或皮膚。
generatedExample: His work-hardened hands showed years of manual labor.

explanationZhTw: 「繭；硬皮」；通常因摩擦或長期使用而形成。
generatedExample: After weeks of climbing, he had callouses on both hands.

explanationZhTw: 這裡表示「突然想到；掠過腦中」；不是字面上的「發生在他身上」。
generatedExample: It occurred to me that I had left my keys at the office.

explanationZhTw: 「難以想像的愚行／荒謬蠢事」；`inconceivable` 強調完全無法接受或想像。
generatedExample: Skipping the safety checks would be inconceivable folly.

explanationZhTw: 這是較文學性的說法，指「擁擠的人群／人潮的壓迫感」；`press` 不是按壓，而是密集擁擠。
generatedExample: We edged forward through the press of bodies at the station.
